finding.description: totalAssets() (152-159) returns getDelegation(vault,operator) - _pendingRedeemAssets, floored to 0 when reserved >= underlying. convertToShares (165-168) = assets*(totalSupply()+1e3)/(totalAssets()+1e3); when totalAssets()==0 with totalSupply()>0 the denominator collapses to the 1e3 virtual offset, so a dust deposit mints astronomically many shares. requestRedeem reserves _pendingRedeemAssets += convertToAssets(shares) and burns shares but does NOT reduce the underlying delegation; a slash (SlashingManager.sol:490 reduces pool.totalAssets -> getDelegation drops) with a redeem pending pushes getDelegation below the pre-slash reserved amount, flooring totalAssets() to 0. ATTACK: holders requestRedeem reserving ~all underlying (supply drops to S', reserved=P); operator gets slashed (natural or attacker-operated); totalAssets()->0; attacker deposit(tiny) mints assets*(S'+1e3)/1e3 shares = majority of supply; when pending redeems claim, reservation releases and totalAssets recovers, attacker now owns nearly the entire vault. OUTCOME: theft of non-redeeming holders' stake plus future deposits/rewards for a dust deposit. The floor-to-0 comment handles underflow but not the share-price collapse.

finding.title: Exiting redeemer over-claims rewards accrued during unbonding; reservedAssets vs actual-returned mismatch dilutes remaining vault holders
finding.description: requestRedeem prices the exit once: assets=convertToAssets(shares) (287), burns liquid shares (295), stores reservedAssets=assets and unstakeShares (308-314), reserves _pendingRedeemAssets += assets (304). scheduleDelegatorUnstake only QUEUES; it does not reduce the vault delegation shares, so those unstakeShares keep earning pool rewards and keep counting in getDelegation(vault) during the bond-less delay. At redeem (400-402) executeDelegatorUnstakeAndWithdraw(unstakeShares) returns _sharesToAmount(unstakeShares) at the CURRENT (reward-inflated) rate, more than reservedAssets, sent to the receiver, but the vault releases only reservedAssets (stale) from _pendingRedeemAssets (393-398). OUTCOME: underlying shrinks by unstakeShares*rate_now while reservation released is the smaller reservedAssets, so remaining holders' totalAssets()=getDelegation-reserved drops by the reward delta; the exiting redeemer pockets rewards on already-burned liquid shares. No reconciliation between reserved and actually-withdrawn assets. Magnitude = reward accrual over the unbonding window, repeatable.

finding.title: Vault approves staking not the adapter: adapter-backed asset deposits revert/mis-account; permissionless factory does no asset validation
finding.description: deposit() forceApprove(staking,assets) then staking.depositERC20(asset,assets) (200-211). In the staking layer _handleErc20Deposit routes through a registered adapter: IAssetAdapter(adapter).deposit(vault,amount) (DepositManager.sol:76), and the adapter safeTransferFrom(vault,adapter,assets) (StandardAssetAdapter.sol:88, RebasingAssetAdapter.sol:119) pulls from the ADAPTER allowance the vault never granted (it approved staking). (1) If an adapter is registered for the vault asset (now or later by governance) every deposit()/mint() reverts -> all live vaults for that asset are silently bricked with no migration path (hard-coded approval target). (2) Rebasing adapters return non-1:1 shares (assets*1e8 scale, RebasingAssetAdapter.sol:130-131): depositERC20 credits dep.amount += adapterShares while the vault delegates the RAW amount (delegateWithOptions line 214) and schedules unstake in raw amount -> units mismatch between liquid-share accounting and staking credit. Aggravating: LiquidDelegationFactory.createVault (76-110) is permissionless with NO asset-enabled/adapter check (declared AssetNotEnabled error never used), and the deterministic vaultKey lets anyone squat the canonical vault for any (operator,asset,blueprints) tuple.

finding.title: RebasingAssetAdapter preview/actual share inconsistency (assetsToShares=assets*1e18 vs deposit mints assets*1e8 on empty pool)
finding.description: On empty pool (totalShares==0) deposit() mints actualReceived*(0+VIRTUAL_SHARES)/(0+VIRTUAL_ASSETS)=actualReceived*1e8 (130-131), but assetsToShares (183-190) returns assets*INITIAL_SHARES_PER_ASSET=assets*1e18 when totalShares==0 and previewDeposit forwards it (193-195). previewDeposit over-reports first-deposit shares by 1e10x versus actual mint. sharesToAssets/previewWithdraw (175-200) use raw currentBalance/totalShares with NO virtual offset, diverging from the offset math in withdraw (151). View functions are not self-consistent with state-changing functions; any on-chain consumer sizing share expectations or slippage off the previews for the bootstrap deposit gets wildly wrong values.

finding.title: [VAULT] mint() rounds required assets DOWN (floor) instead of UP → minter underpays, dilutes existing holders
finding.description:
SETUP: `mint(shares, receiver)` (226-243) computes the assets the minter must pay as `assets = convertToAssets(shares)` (229). `convertToAssets` (174-177) is `shares.mulDiv(totalAssets()+1e3, totalSupply()+1e3, Math.Rounding.Floor)` — it rounds DOWN. The minter then transfers exactly that floored `assets`, delegates it, and is minted the full requested `shares` (240).

BUG: ERC-4626 (and basic vault safety) requires `mint()` to round the asset cost UP, so the minter never receives more shares than they pay for. Here the floor means the minter pays `floor(shares * rate)` for `shares` whose fair cost is `ceil(shares * rate)`. The deposit() path is correct (it floors shares, favoring the vault), but mint() floors the asset cost, favoring the minter. The two paths round in opposite, asymmetric directions.

ATTACK / OUTCOME: A minter always gets the rounding remainder (up to <1 asset-unit of the current exchange rate) for free, sourced from the existing share holders' backing (the vault delegates fewer assets than the minted shares represent, so totalAssets grows by less than the implied per-share value). When the exchange rate is high (post-rewards, rate ≫ 1), the per-call edge is the fractional part of `shares*rate`, i.e. up to nearly `rate` asset-units, captured for free and repeatable. Magnitude per call is bounded by one rate-unit so impact is dust-to-small (hence low severity), but it is a genuine spec violation and a systematic, attacker-preferable rounding bias (use mint() instead of deposit() to always capture the edge). Fix: compute the mint cost with `Math.Rounding.Ceil` (a `convertToAssetsRoundUp` helper) so the minter pays at least the fair asset amount.

finding.title: [rounding] LiquidDelegationVault.mint() floors required assets (ERC4626 mint must round up) → underpaid shares
finding.description:
SETUP: `mint(shares, receiver)` computes the assets the caller must pay as `assets = convertToAssets(shares)` (line 229). `convertToAssets` (174-177) rounds DOWN (Math.Rounding.Floor): assets = shares * (totalAssets()+1e3) / (totalSupply()+1e3). The vault then pulls exactly `assets` and mints exactly `shares`.

BUG: Per ERC-4626, `deposit` must round shares DOWN (done correctly at line 196) but `mint` must round the required assets UP, so the minter always pays at least the fair value of the shares it receives. Here `mint` uses the same floor-rounding conversion as `deposit`/`redeem`. Because of the floor, the minter pays up to (denominator-1)/denominator < 1 asset-unit LESS than the shares are actually worth, yet receives the full `shares`. The shortfall is socialized onto existing holders (the asset-per-share rate ticks down by the rounding dust on every mint).

ATTACK / OUTCOME: An adversary can repeatedly call `mint(shares, self)` choosing `shares` such that `convertToAssets(shares)` rounds down maximally, each call extracting up to ~1 asset-unit of value from existing holders (round-trip mint→requestRedeem). Per-call profit is sub-1-unit so this is a precision/dilution bug, not a drain — but it is a real rounding-direction inversion versus the ERC-4626 invariant the rest of the contract follows (deposit floors shares, requestRedeem floors assets — both pool-favourable; mint floors assets — minter-favourable). Fix: add a ceil-rounding `_convertToAssetsRoundUp` for `mint`.

finding.title: LiquidDelegationVault.mint() rounds required assets DOWN instead of UP -> minter underpays, dilutes holders
finding.description: mint(shares) sets assets=convertToAssets(shares) which uses Math.Rounding.Floor (174-177), then mints the full `shares` for that floored asset cost (229,240). ERC-4626 requires mint() to round the asset cost UP so the minter never gets more shares than paid for. deposit() correctly floors shares (favoring vault) but mint() floors the asset cost (favoring minter) - asymmetric, attacker-preferable rounding. Per-call edge is the fractional part of shares*rate (up to ~1 rate-unit, larger when rate>>1 post-rewards), captured free from existing holders' backing, repeatable. Dust magnitude -> low, but a real spec violation. Fix: compute mint cost with Math.Rounding.Ceil.

finding.title: [accounting] RebasingAssetAdapter preview/view math diverges from executable deposit/withdraw share math
finding.description:
SETUP: RebasingAssetAdapter has two parallel sets of share math: the EXECUTABLE path (deposit line 130-131, withdraw line 150-151) uses the virtual offset (VIRTUAL_SHARES=1e8, VIRTUAL_ASSETS=1); the VIEW/preview path does NOT.

DIVERGENCE 1 (bootstrap factor 1e8 vs 1e18): On the first deposit (totalShares==0, balanceBefore==0), `deposit` mints actualReceived*(0+1e8)/(0+1) = actualReceived*1e8 shares. But `assetsToShares`/`previewDeposit` (183-195) return `assets * INITIAL_SHARES_PER_ASSET = assets*1e18` when totalShares==0 — a 1e10x disagreement with what deposit actually mints.

DIVERGENCE 2 (missing offset in sharesToAssets): `sharesToAssets`/`previewWithdraw` (175-200) compute `shares*balance/totalShares` with NO virtual offset, while the executable `withdraw` (150-151) computes `shares*(balance+1)/(totalShares+1e8)`. The two return different asset amounts for the same shares.

ATTACK / OUTCOME: `DepositManager._sharesToAssets` (DepositManager.sol:305-308) — used to value delegator positions/withdrawals for adapter-backed assets — calls the offset-less `sharesToAssets`, so on-chain accounting/UX reads a different asset value than `withdraw` will actually pay out. Integrations and the staking layer relying on previewDeposit/previewWithdraw/sharesToAssets mis-price rebasing-adapter positions (over- or under-stating by the 1e8/1e18 factor and the offset delta). No direct theft, but the preview/quote surface is unsound; combined with the adapter unit-mismatch already filed it compounds vault mispricing. Fix: make the view functions mirror the offset math and the 1e8 bootstrap exactly.
